outperforms_others_because.best_mean_reward: Exp1 achieved -23.57 mean reward — better than all other experiments which scored -23.71 or worse.
outperforms_others_because.best_max_reward: Exp1 is the only experiment that ever scored -22.0 in a single episode. All others peaked at -23.0 or -24.0.
outperforms_others_because.highest_reward_variance: Exp1 has the highest std_reward (0.73), meaning the agent occasionally discovered better actions — a sign of early learning signal that others lacked entirely (Exp2, Exp4, Exp5 had std=0.0, meaning the agent was stuck at -24 every single episode).
outperforms_others_because.stable_learning_rate: lr=0.0001 is the DQN sweet spot — high enough to update meaningfully, low enough to avoid divergence. Exp2 (lr=0.001) was 10x higher and achieved zero variance, indicating the Q-network destabilised.
outperforms_others_because.optimal_gamma: gamma=0.99 balances short and long-term rewards. Exp4 (gamma=0.90) was too short-sighted and scored -24.0 every episode. Exp5 (gamma=0.999) over-weighted distant rewards and also stagnated at -24.0.
outperforms_others_because.efficient_batch_size: batch=32 gives enough gradient signal without being too noisy (batch=16, Exp7) or too slow to update (batch=128, Exp6 took 247s vs 158s).

worst_configs.reason: These three experiments scored -24.0 mean and -24.0 max with zero variance — the agent lost every single point in every episode. The Q-network either diverged (high LR) or failed to value future rewards correctly (extreme gamma values).
